title: When falling housing prices are good news — and when they're not
description: Denver renters are celebrating falling housing costs. But sometimes cheaper housing is a sign of economic decline. How can you tell the difference?

title: How Trump's 'Complete and Total Endorsement' has reshaped the Republican Party
description: An NPR analysis of more than a thousand Trump endorsements in House, Senate and governor races over the last decade finds the president now picks candidates earlier — and in safer races.

title: As Republican Party looks to future without Trump in office, Utah could be a road map
description: In 2028, President Trump will not be on the ballot, leaving Republicans to decide the future of the party. Utah — which has a complicated relationship with the president — could be a starting point.

title: This man is a bus driver and grandfather. A Supreme Court ruling could reimprison him
description: Anthony Bailey's case is one of about a dozen that could be directly affected by a Supreme Court ruling limiting how prisoners can use the compassionate release program to get out early.

title: After losing jobs for posting about Charlie Kirk, some are getting 6-figure payouts
description: While workers, who were employees in government or public institutions, feel vindicated by how their lawsuits concluded, they are still grappling with the aftermath.

title: Trump to tout his economic gains during Pennsylvania visit
description: President Trump visits Pennsylvania's Lehigh Valley Tuesday, hoping to tout the economy in the wake of an initial agreement with Iran.

title: Will Trump's pivot to the economy help soothe Republican voters ahead of midterms?
description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Ron Bonjean, longtime spokesperson for multiple House and Senate Republicans, about President Trump's pivot to the economy in Pennsylvania Tuesday.

title: New York's primary will test NYC mayor's political power
description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani faces the biggest test of his political power since taking office with Tuesday's congressional primaries. Voters will decide if they back candidates he endorsed.

title: Trump supporter’s company pledges $1 million to fix White House lawn after UFC event
description: Ohio-based ScottsMiracle-Gro said it is donating “a combination of monetary and product support.” A government watchdog said the arrangement raises ethics questions.

title: A federal judge finds a Trump data system to verify voters is unlawful
description: Tens of millions of voters have had their data run through the Trump administration's revamped SAVE tool. A judge just found it unlawful.

title: Vance heads back to U.S. after positive start to U.S.-Iran talks
description: Vice President Vance is on his way back to the U.S. after he said negotiators made progress toward a lasting peace deal.
